metadata.notes: First experiment. Installer discovered as Makeself archive. Device limit status unknown.
First device-limit hit. Confirmed env-var auth. Slot consumed, later cleared by operator.
First complete install with cleared slot. vnstock_data 3.0.0 installed. Sandbox-1.
Second container on same host. Different device ID. Hit device limit. Proved IDs are not deterministic.
Tested with vnai/pandas/numpy pre-installed. Hit device limit.
Tested with requests+pandas only. HOME override confirmed. Hit device limit.
Tested vendor troubleshooting guide one-liner. Hit device limit.
Full install succeeded. vnstock_data 3.0.0. Second container test hit limit immediately after.
Script skipped because vnstock_data already importable. No installer executed.
Tested vendor extra-index-url. No installer run. No slot consumed.
Operator attempted forced re-registration by renaming vnstock_data. Installer consumed slot then timed out. Product restored from backup.

Re-ran current install-vnstock.sh in clean sandbox after operator cleared all slots. Device registration succeeded (visible in UI at 00:29:51). Sponsor package download failed. Asymmetric failure: slot consumed despite "failure" message. BACKGROUND TASK: bash-mwk3v66i

During script rewrite idempotency check, `.venv/bin/python -c "import vnstock_data"` triggered vendor re-authentication (auth cache expired since 2026-05-11). Vendor backend restored previously cleared device to visible dashboard. Key finding: vendor "clear" is a soft delete; re-auth restores hidden devices. No installer executed. No new slot consumed, but visible count increased.

Phase 2 validation of rewritten install-vnstock.sh in clean Docker sandbox. Script pre-flight passed, installer downloaded/verified, device registered in step 5. Step 6 failed: device limit exceeded. Root cause: a previously cleared device had been reactivated by an accidental `import vnstock_data` call before the test. The reactivated device counts as occupied, blocking new registrations. This confirms vendor "clear" is a soft delete; re-auth restores hidden devices. Script defensive features validated: error interception, atomicity guard, next steps. Script bug found and fixed: pre-flight only checked system Python for requests, but run_installer prepends venv to PATH. Added venv Python requests check.

Phase 2 happy path validation of rewritten install-vnstock.sh with bug fixes. Operator cleared devices before run. Installer exited 0, device registered (bronze tier, 1/1 devices used), vnstock_data downloaded and extracted. Script post-flight import check FAILED due to HOME env var not preserved: vnstock_data reads config from $HOME/.vnstock during import, but post-flight check ran with default HOME=/root instead of API_HOME. Root cause identified and fixed: all vnstock_data import checks now set HOME=API_HOME. API ping test has same bug and was also fixed. Slot consumed: 1 (happy path installer registration succeeded). Idempotency and --force tests blocked pending device clearance.

Diagnostic container run on same host immediately after happy path. Generated identical device fingerprint (same kernel/host). Installer reached step 6 and failed with device limit exceeded. Vendor blocked sponsor package download but did NOT create a new device (same ID). No additional slot consumed. Confirmed script error interception, atomicity guard, and next steps all work. Container state inspection showed no vnstock_data in site-packages (expected since step 6 failed before package install).

Validation run of rewritten install-vnstock.sh with stale-container guard. Fresh Docker container, named volume for .venv isolation. Install script exited 0, device registered (bronze tier, 1/1 used). vnstock_data installed and importable. API ping returned warning (non-fatal). Stale-container guard did not trigger (fresh container, no pre-existing .vnstock). Budget gate (validation_window_active) verified working.

Phase-04 revalidation after operator sudo-cleanup and re-bootstrap. Device registered (bronze tier, 1/1 used). vnstock_data 3.1.8 installed. All 5 capability scripts failed with vendor auth error. Vendor portal confirms device visible with activity at 00:42:35. Root cause: vendor account/subscription authorization, not registration.

metadata.note: Re-pinned per inbound-gate protocol. The user's recent message (debug mastracode install failure) is a legitimate operator state-change unrelated to vnstock substrate. vnstock sidecar remains unmodified.

Re-pinned per inbound-gate protocol with correct affected_system (vnstock, not runtime-state). Staleness check uses AFFECTED_SYSTEM_TO_CONSTRAINTS map in tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/file-readers.js which only recognizes vnstock. Operator's current work is Phase E Plan 4 (mastracode install); this re-pin unblocks that work without consuming any vnstock slot.

Re-pinned per inbound-gate protocol. Operator's current work (PR #28 fix: feed c8 Istanbul coverage to Fallow --coverage, local pre-commit + CI consistency) is unrelated to the vnstock substrate. No vnstock slot consumed; vnstock sidecar unmodified.
